title: Insecure Direct Object Reference
description: An endpoint that allows users to view their profile by ID.

stages.name: Find the vulnerability
stages.instruction: Identify the logic flaw in this endpoint.
stages.target: The application trusts the 'user_id' provided in the URL and does not check if the current user has permission to view that profile.

stages.name: Exploit it
stages.instruction: If your user ID is 5, how would you try to view user 1's profile?

stages.name: Patch it
stages.instruction: Provide a conceptual fix for this endpoint.
stages.target: Check if the requested user_id matches the session user_id or if the user has administrative privileges.

stages.name: Explain it
stages.instruction: Explain the importance of horizontal vs vertical access control.
stages.target: Horizontal access control prevents users from accessing data of other users at the same level. Vertical access control prevents users from accessing data of users at a higher level (e.g., admins).

hints: What happens if you change the ID in the URL?
Does the code check who is logged in?
